title: Heleochloa setulosa
taxonomy: Heleochloa setulosa is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to Australia and is often found growing in moist environments, including swamps and riverbanks. This species is part of the subfamily Chloridoideae, which is one of the largest subfamilies within the Poaceae family. The classification of Heleochloa setulosa is further detailed at the genus and species levels, with Heleochloa being the genus and setulosa being the species. Through this classification, Heleochloa setulosa is identified as a unique and distinct species within the plant kingdom.

intro: Heleochloa setulosa, also known as Swallen's hair grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, headaches, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Heleochloa setulosa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
